mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is positive only when the policy rate is in a sustained downtrend; a flat or rising rate path should not produce the same return pattern.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its recent trend, the market infers tighter future policy and lower real valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest around inflation surprises that exceed the prior 6-month pace.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal sign flips in more than one major rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and slower demand, which lowers expected earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism becomes active when UNRATE rises materially relative to its recent baseline, not when it is merely high.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in a majority of instances after a 0.3pp 3-month rise in UNRATE, or if the trigger has no predictive lift versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth and industrial production rising together indicate broad real-economy momentum that supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint condition matters because strong payrolls without output growth, or output growth without hiring, is a weaker equity signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-and-INDPRO growth condition does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in triggered months more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS indicates stronger current consumption and revenue growth expectations, which lifts equities over the next 20 trading days. The signal is activated only when retail sales growth clears a meaningful acceleration threshold from its recent trend.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate exceeds 6-month growth rate by at least 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if retail-sales acceleration triggers do not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not above the unconditional mean by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading demand indicator; a sustained decline in HOUST reflects weaker construction, softer ancillary demand, and lower cyclical earnings expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is negative when the housing trend is falling rather than merely low.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in months with a persistent HOUST downtrend, or if HOUST has no incremental explanatory power after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production weakens while unemployment rises, the economy is transitioning into a supply-and-demand contraction that compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal depends on the joint deterioration, not either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO decline plus UNRATE rise does not outperform single-series signals, or if SPX returns are positive in most joint-deterioration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, the combination of CPIAUCSL running hot and FEDFUNDS staying elevated predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because real-rate pressure and policy-risk repricing intensify simultaneously. This regime-specific effect should weaken outside tightening cycles.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-plus-high-rate condition fails to produce negative 5-day SPX returns in tightening regimes, or if the effect persists equally in easing regimes.
